name: Network & Zero Trust — Zscaler + Palo Alto NGFW
description: ATT&CK technique coverage for network security and Zero Trust vendors: Zscaler Zero Trust Exchange and Palo Alto Networks NGFW. Coverage via NIST 800-53 controls AC-17, SC-7, SC-8, AC-4, AC-20. Source: https://github.com/TeamStarWolf/TeamStarWolf/blob/main/data/vendor_to_technique.jsonl

techniques.comment: Zscaler ZTNA — AC-17: Zero Trust Network Access replaces VPN, requires explicit authorization for every remote service connection
Zscaler — Remote Desktop Protocol access controlled via ZTNA policy
Zscaler — SMB/Windows Admin Shares access controlled via micro-segmentation
Zscaler — SSH access controlled via ZTNA policy
Zscaler ZTNA — AC-17: external remote services replaced by identity-verified, device-verified ZTNA tunnels
Zscaler + Palo Alto — SC-7/AC-4: SSL inspection and DLP block exfiltration over alternative protocols
Zscaler + Palo Alto — Exfiltration over asymmetric encrypted non-C2 blocked by SSL inspection
Zscaler + Palo Alto — Exfiltration over unencrypted non-C2 protocol blocked by policy
Zscaler + Palo Alto — SC-7: C2 over application layer protocols detected via SSL inspection and threat intelligence
Zscaler + Palo Alto — C2 over HTTP/S detection
Zscaler + Palo Alto — C2 over DNS detection
Palo Alto NGFW — SC-8: encrypted network traffic prevents network sniffing; TLS inspection detects cleartext credential transmission
Palo Alto NGFW — SC-8: ARP poisoning and MITM attack detection on network segments
Zscaler + Palo Alto — SC-7: proxy and relay traffic inspection; Tor exit node blocking
Zscaler + Palo Alto — Multi-hop proxy / Tor blocking via threat intelligence
Palo Alto NGFW — SC-7: non-standard protocol detection via App-ID
Zscaler + Palo Alto — SC-7: protocol tunneling detection (DNS tunneling, ICMP tunneling)
Palo Alto NGFW — SC-7: non-standard port usage detection via App-ID regardless of port
Zscaler + Palo Alto — SC-7/AC-4: exfiltration over C2 channel detected and blocked
Zscaler CASB — AC-20: cloud account data transfer monitoring and blocking
